Im currently making a summary video about morimen's universe before I retell the story in each chapter
and I just gotta make sure I get the data right

If I remember correctly, once someone is completely been dissolution'd they are no longer remembered by anyone in the world
and have their entire body transformed into monsters right? And if that monster lives long enough it becomes a beast that can eventually cause a D rift event?
(lily's story with koom) But if someone dies before transforming while having dissolution, they sometime will leave behind silver cores instead.
While the dissolution is happening, people will also start to forget but not completely.

Am I correct?
Im just unsure if the beast thing is correct or not or if that's just a Ultra dimension exclusive issue
coz that's the only time the devs referenced cerberus
I am not the person to ask for lore, I only read half of it and understood half of it. From what I know, aigis dissolution'd and respawn back at bed.

In her case, she was a half awaker due to the implant that they put in her brain, 
until she decided to off her self in order to save everyone from her going full berzerk.
Eventually they found her core and awoke her and she regains her memory back.
she only managed to come back because of the second consciousness that the researchers put inside her brain
And no memory of her were lost?
yes, because jenkins still remembered her I think
she didn't completely contract dissolution If i remember correctly
the same way brown didn't forget jenkins when she died
she died due to the D-slime but not because of dissolution itself
either that or animals do not forget, but I doubt it
oh no , my bad she is indeed forgotten, that's literally her wish when she jumped

i guess brown is just special,
addendum, it seems like there is a certain amount of time before the memory is completely erased which is why 24 and brown still remembers her

if dissoluted 
=> 1. corrode into dust
=> 2. or awakers

you can read the archive
they have some information
so when does the monster transformation begin in the cycle actually

From my understanding, 
Person contracts Dissolution -> brain begins to fill with D-slime -> Loss of brain causes change in mental state
-> D-slime wound appears on the body -> Patient starts to see phantasms -> Insanity -> Transformation into monster
-> Dissolution completes -> memory is forgotten after a month or two
The reason I Was wondering this is because 24 transformed and jenkins was starting to forget her sister
but 24 didn't corrode into dust
the memory loss itself comes after a person has been completely dissoluted right?
Because if it turns into dust eventually then we wouldn't really need to fight anything
seems like the game's just a bit vague here, I'll go back do some more research

You'd disappear physically and mentally when completely dissoluted, even no trace of silver will remain. It's practically impossible to prove your existence at this point since all of your history as well as your proof of existence is also corroded accordingly.

The Monster phase is just a stage on which the target hasn't completely dissoluted, thus if they get killed, their silver remains could still be found. This was the case for most of the deaths in the series.

If Im not wrong, gnostic index detect the spiritual power inside of a silver. It also determine whether a silver is worth or could be awakened based on its index or not.

It might be because the silver Jenkins drop have more than average gnostic index than other silver, results in how other people can still remember her.

Anyhow, as long as they dropped silver upon death, it means that they aren't completely dissoluted. When they aren't completely dissoluted, it means that the probability of someone remember them is still high. The same case is also true with Celeste and Helton. Cmiiw.

<@356805227904499713>  Read over this for me fam
This is like a short story I wanna put in before I explain the mechanics of dissolution.

~ patient -.---.- treament record ~

The patient was recently admitted into the psychological ward for symptoms of hysteria and delirium, apparently being able to see and hear things that no one else can perceive
Mr.-.--.- has no records of prior neuropathological diseases, he lived a simple and normal life with a loving family. Until one day, according to the patient's family, Mr.-.-.---.- began
to have frequent mood swings, hallucination episodes and cases of insomnia. We believe that his lack of sleep might be the reason for the deterioration of his mental health.
The medications given to the patient seems to be ineffective and as such he is within our care until further notice.

Week 1 of treatment

Because the patient had been living a relatively normal life prior to admission, he was visited by friend and coworkers to check on how he is recovering. But eventually, the visits became less frequent and stopped within less than three days of admission. This might be a cause of his disease. Eventually no one but the patient's close family visited him. Signs of his mental deterioration does not show any signs of slowing down.

Week 2 of treatment 

It seems the patient's family has stopped visiting. Perhaps some kind of real life work had them occupied? There is no way a family this loving could forget their loved one in the hospital.
On an unrelated note, despite doing my ward rounds every day, I seem to have a hard time remembering the patient's name. I only call him patient because that's all I remember of him.
It is my professionalism that is keeping everything intact right now, every time I look at the patient's files, the letters seems to be.. Blurry and shuffles around everytime I try to focus on the name. I am noting this down in case my stress is causing my work ability to falter.

Doctor's diary

There seems to be a little girl, the age of ten who's been coming around every single day, asking me about her father which she swears is under my care. I looked up every single file that I could and I couldn't find a single description matching what the little girl was saying. She said she can't say the name properly but she knows what the person looked like. She said that her father came in for severe symptoms like hysteria and delirium, surely a case that severe wouldn't be something I can easily forget. Recently there has been reports of screaming and scratching noises coming from the psych ward. But no matter how much everyone searched, we were unable to determine the source of the voice. I can feel it, something is definitely going to happen. I should get security to prevent the girl from coming into the hospital in search of a stranger nobody knows about.

Day ???/??/???

Everything is full of blood, corpses of the patient lay haphazardly on the floor. Bodies shredded beyond recognition. What happened here?
There is no way we have someone unstable enough nor strong enough to make this happen... I walked through the scene of carnage trying to find a toilet so I can hurl the contents of my stomach. As I reached for the sink, I looked at the mirror and saw an unfamiliar triangle mark on my skin... Just what is happening to me?
